title: Freeze-on-Death with Loss Reason Display
content:
## Overview When any snake dies, the game enters a **death freeze** instead of immediately jumping to the Game Over screen. The board stays visible and frozen, the fatal collision cell is highlighted in amber, a pulsing banner names the loss reason, and the game waits for any key press before advancing to the normal Game Over screen. This applies to all modes: single-player human, bot-driven single-player, and bot-vs-bot. ## Motivation Previously, the instant a snake died, `endGame()` / `endGameTwoPlayer()` called `showScreen('game-over')`, which set `display:none` on `#game` and hid the canvas entirely. The fatal head cell was never even drawn — collision is checked before `this.snake.unshift(head)`, and `draw()` was never called on the death path. Players had no way to see how they lost. ## Architecture ### Deferred callback pattern The freeze inserts itself as a step in front of the existing end-game functions rather than rewriting them. `beginDeathFreeze(finalize: () => void)` stores the end-game function as a callback. All winner resolution, high-score saving, and leaderboard logic in `endGame()` / `endGameTwoPlayer()` runs unchanged when the freeze ends. The freeze is purely additive. ### New instance fields (`src/main.ts`) | Field | Type | Purpose | |---|---|---| | `deathReason` | `'wall' \| 'self' \| 'opponent' \| 'head-to-head' \| 'starved'` | Banner-facing loss reason | | `deathCells` | `Array<{ x: number; y: number; who: 1 \| 2 }>` | Fatal head cell(s) to highlight | | `awaitingDeathAck` | `boolean` | True during the freeze | | `pendingFinalize` | `(() => void) \| null` | Deferred end-game callback | The existing `deathCause: 'collision' | 'starved'` field is unchanged — it still drives the Game Over title and winner nuance logic. All four fields are reset in `startNewGame` alongside `this.deathCause = 'collision'`, and `hideDeathBanner()` is called there too, so a restart never leaves a stuck banner or stale highlight cell. ### `beginDeathFreeze(finalize)` method 1. Sets `isGameOver = true`, `awaitingDeathAck = true`, `pendingFinalize = finalize`. 2. Calls `stopAutoRotate()` and clears the game loop interval (idempotent when `endGame` re-runs the same lines at finalize time). 3. Hides `#starvation-warning` so it doesn't overlap the banner. 4. Calls `this.draw()` to render the frozen board with the death cell highlighted. 5. Calls `this.showDeathBanner()`. ### Four death sites rewired (`src/main.ts`) Each site sets `deathReason` and `deathCells`, then calls `beginDeathFreeze(() => this.endGame())` or `beginDeathFreeze(() => this.endGameTwoPlayer(p1Dead, p2Dead))` instead of calling the end-game function directly: - **Single-player collision:** `deathReason = this.inBounds(head) ? 'self' : 'wall'`; `deathCells = [{x: head.x, y: head.y, who: 1}]`. - **Single-player starvation:** `deathReason = 'starved'`; `deathCells = []`. - **Two-player collision:** pushes `head1` (who:1) if `p1Dead`, `head2` (who:2) if `p2Dead`; derives `deathReason` from the already-computed collision booleans (`headToHead ? 'head-to-head' : wall ? 'wall' : self ? 'self' : 'opponent'`). - **Two-player starvation:** `deathReason = 'starved'`; `deathCells = []`. ### Death cell rendering in `draw()` Only active when `awaitingDeathAck`. The amber death cell is pushed into the painter's-algorithm object list as a `'death'` type entry before sorting, so it is drawn at its correct depth relative to surrounding segments. Array sort stability ensures the marker paints over the segment sharing its cell. **Bounds handling** branches on the grid rectangle (`x < 0 || x >= gridSize || y < 0 || y >= gridSize`): - **Off the grid rectangle (wall death):** the true cell projects mostly off-canvas. The marker is drawn at the snake's last valid head position (`snake[0]` / `snake2[0]`), and a sliver is also drawn at the true out-of-bounds coordinate so it pokes past the border edge as an "off the edge" cue. This sliver is drawn after the sorted pass as a special case (it has no meaningful depth position in the scene). - **Within the grid rectangle (self / opponent / head-to-head, or Iron Snake interior void):** projects on-canvas; drawn at its correct sorted depth. Starvation (`deathCells = []`) is a no-op for this block — the freeze shows the last live frame plus the banner only. Death cells are re-read on every `draw()` call, so the highlight persists across window resizes and any redraws that occur while frozen. The `drawBlockShadow` calls and duplicated `drawBlock` calls from an earlier on-top rendering approach were removed when the painter's-sort integration was added. ### Banner DOM and CSS - **`index.html`:** `
` is added inside `.canvas-wrap` as a sibling after `#starvation-warning`. It overlays the canvas without shifting layout (same technique as the pause overlay). - **`src/style.css`:** `.death-banner` is modeled on `.starvation-warning` but centered (`top: 50%; transform: translate(-50%, -50%)`), larger, alarm-red, `pointer-events: none`, with its own pulse keyframe. `showDeathBanner()` builds text from `deathReason` (with P1/P2 labels in two-player mode) and appends " — Press any key", then unhides the element. `hideDeathBanner()` re-hides it. Banner copy: | Reason | Text | |---|---| | `wall` | "Hit the wall!" | | `self` | "Ran into itself!" | | `opponent` | "Crashed into the other snake!" | | `head-to-head` | "Head-on collision!" | | `starved` | "Starved!" | ### Acknowledgement branch in `handleKeyPress` Added at the very top of `handleKeyPress` in `src/main.ts`, after the INPUT/TEXTAREA/SELECT guard and before the Enter/r/Escape branches: ```typescript if (this.awaitingDeathAck) { if (e.repeat || e.key === 'Shift' || e.key === 'Control' || e.key === 'Alt' || e.key === 'Meta') return e.preventDefault() this.awaitingDeathAck = false this.hideDeathBanner() const finalize = this.pendingFinalize this.pendingFinalize = null finalize?.() return } ``` **Any key dismisses the freeze** — including camera keys (rotate, overhead, perspective). Bare modifier keys and key-repeat events are ignored to prevent a stray held key from skipping the freeze instantly. After `finalize()` runs, `showScreen('game-over')` fires and normal Game Over key handling (Enter/R to restart) resumes unchanged. ## Interaction with other systems - **High-score / leaderboard timing:** `saveHighScore`, `maybePromptForEntry`, and the bvb `addEntry` calls fire at ack-time rather than at the death instant. Nothing depends on them firing earlier. Closing the tab during the freeze skips recording — acceptable. - **Iron Snake:** interior void cells are within the grid rectangle and render correctly at sorted depth. - **Head and tail graphics:** the frozen board renders with the full head (1.6× height, direction-facing eyes) and tapered tail introduced in commit 5247d42b. The painter's-algorithm object list classifies the last segment of each snake as `'tail'`/`'tail2'` and routes it through `drawSnakeTail()`, which uses the shared `drawPrism` primitive for consistent shading. - **Two-player simultaneous death (draw / head-to-head):** both fatal head cells are pushed into `deathCells` and both highlight simultaneously. ## Files - `src/main.ts` — new fields, resets, `beginDeathFreeze`, `showDeathBanner`/`hideDeathBanner`, four death sites, death-cell rendering in `draw()`, ack branch in `handleKeyPress`. - `index.html` — `#death-banner` element inside `.canvas-wrap`. - `src/style.css` — `.death-banner` styles and pulse keyframe.
